1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)

52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
5	from .formatter import format_table, format_json
6	
7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI tool (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to search bookmarks. A comprehensive test suite already exists at `tests/test_search.py` (17 tests) that defines the exact contract for the search module and CLI integration. The goal is to implement the code that makes all those tests pass while keeping the 11 existing storage tests green.

---

## Step 1: Create `src/search.py`

Create a new file `src/search.py` with four functions. Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` to extract domains from URLs.

### `search_by_title(bookmarks, query)`
- Return bookmarks where `query.lower()` is a substring of `bookmark.title.lower()`
- Returns `[]` when no match

### `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)`
- Return bookmarks where `tag` appears in `bookmark.tags` (exact string match)
- Returns `[]` when no match

### `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)`
- Extract the hostname from each bookmark's URL via `urlparse(bookmark.url).hostname`
- Return bookmarks where `domain` is found **in** the hostname string (substring match — needed because `"docs.python.org"` must match the full hostname `"docs.python.org"`, and `"github.com"` must match `"github.com"`)
- Returns `[]` when no match

### `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None)`
- If **no** criteria are provided, return all bookmarks (test: `test_no_criteria_returns_all`)
- Start with the full list and progressively filter:
  1. If `title` is not None → filter with `search_by_title`
  2. If `tag` is not None → filter with `search_by_tag`
  3. If `domain` is not None → filter with `search_by_domain`
- This implements AND logic naturally

### Implementation skeleton

```python
from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    query_lower = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if query_lower in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    return [b for b in bookmarks if domain in (urlparse(b.url).hostname or "")]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results
```

---

## Step 2: Add `search` subcommand to `src/cli.py`

### 2a. Add import

At the top of `src/cli.py`, add:
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b. Add parser in `build_parser()`

After the `show` subparser block (line 39), add a new `search` subparser:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (substring)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Search by tag (exact match)")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Search by URL domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

This matches the test expectations:
- `parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])` → `args.command == "search"`, `args.title == "python"`
- `parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])` → `args.tag == "docs"`, `args.format == "json"`

### 2c. Add command handler in `main()`

After the `elif args.command == "show":` block, add:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

---

## Files to Modify

| File | Action | Lines affected |
|------|--------|---------------|
| `src/search.py` | **Create** (new file) | ~30 lines |
| `src/cli.py` | **Edit** — add import, search parser, search handler | ~20 lines added |

No changes to `src/models.py`, `src/storage.py`, `src/formatter.py`, or any test files.

---

## Verification

Run all tests from the project root:

```bash
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

**Expected results:**
- `tests/test_storage.py` — 11 tests pass (unchanged)
- `tests/test_search.py` — 17 tests pass (newly passing)
- **Total: 28 tests, 0 failures**

8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")

10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:

## Comprehensive Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775383014596-hq3l67`

The project is a Python CLI tool for managing bookmarks with the following structure:

```
.
├── PLAN.md                      (empty)
├── pyproject.toml              (project config)
├── .gitignore
├── .git
├── .venv                        (symlink to virtual environment)
├── src/
│   ├── __init__.py             (empty)
│   ├── models.py               (data model)
│   ├── storage.py              (persistence layer)
│   ├── cli.py                  (CLI command structure)
│   ├── formatter.py            (output formatting)
│   └── bookmark_cli.egg-info/  (package metadata)
└── tests/
    ├── __init__.py             (empty)
    ├── test_storage.py         (storage tests)
    └── test_search.py          (search feature tests - importing non-existent search module)
```

---

### 2. DATA MODEL (src/models.py)

The `Bookmark` class is a dataclass with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `id` | `str` | N/A (required) | Unique identifier for the bookmark |
| `url` | `str` | N/A (required) | The URL being bookmarked |
| `title` | `str` | N/A (required) | Display title for the bookmark |
| `tags` | `List[str]` | `[]` (empty list) | Searchable tags/categories |
| `notes` | `str` | `""` (empty string) | Additional notes about the bookmark |
| `created_at` | `str` | ISO format timestamp | ISO format datetime string |

**Methods:**
- `to_dict()`: Converts bookmark to dictionary for JSON serialization
- `from_dict(data)`: Class method to reconstruct bookmark from dictionary

---

### 3. CLI STRUCTURE (src/cli.py)

The CLI is structured using Python's `argparse` library with 5 subcommands:

**Parser builder function:** `build_parser()` - Returns an ArgumentParser with:

| Command | Arguments | Options | Description |
|---------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `add` | `url`, `title` | `--tags` (nargs='*'), `--notes` | Add a new bookmark |
| `list` | N/A | `--format` (choices: table/json, default: table) | List all bookmarks |
| `delete` | `id` | N/A | Delete a bookmark by ID |
| `show` | `id` | N/A | Show a single bookmark |
| ~~`search`~~ | ~~TBD~~ | ~~TBD~~ | **NOT YET IMPLEMENTED** - Tests define expected behavior |

**Main function:** `main(argv=None)`
- Parses arguments
- Routes to appropriate command handler
- Handles all command logic inline (add, list, delete, show)
- Returns exit code 1 for errors

**Command Handlers:**
- **add**: Creates bookmark with uuid, prints confirmation message with ID
- **list**: Loads bookmarks, formats as table (default) or JSON, prints output
- **delete**: Deletes by ID, prints success/failure message
- **show**: Gets single bookmark, displays as formatted table

---

### 4. EXISTING COMMANDS & OUTPUT FORMATTING (src/formatter.py)

**Two output formats supported:**

**`format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str`**
- Fixed-width columns: ID (8), Title (30), URL (40), Tags (20)
- Shows first 8 chars of UUID as "ID"
- Returns "No bookmarks found." if empty
- Column header with separator line
- Truncates long values to column width

**`format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str`**
- Uses `json.dumps()` with indent=2
- Converts each bookmark to dict via `to_dict()`
- Pretty-printed JSON output

---

### 5. STORAGE LAYER (src/storage.py)

**Storage mechanism:** JSON file at `~/.bookmarks.json` (DEFAULT_DB_PATH)

**Key functions:**

| Function | Parameters | Returns | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|---------|---------|
| `load_bookmarks(db_path)` | db_path | `List[Bookmark]` | Loads all bookmarks from file, returns empty list if file missing |
| `save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)` | bookmarks, db_path | None | Persists bookmarks to JSON file with indent=2 |
| `add_bookmark(url, title, tags, notes, db_path)` | url, title, tags (optional), notes, db_path | `Bookmark` | Creates new bookmark with UUID, appends to file, returns created bookmark |
| `delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` | bookmark_id, db_path | `bool` | Removes bookmark by ID, returns True if found and deleted |
| `get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` | bookmark_id, db_path | `Optional[Bookmark]` | Retrieves single bookmark by ID |

**Key implementation details:**
- IDs are generated as `str(uuid.uuid4())`
- All functions support custom `db_path` for testing
- Simple list filtering for delete/get operations

---

### 6. TEST STRUCTURE

**Test File 1: tests/test_storage.py**

Tests the storage layer with 5 test classes:

1. **TestLoadBookmarks** (2 tests)
   - Empty file handling
   - Loading bookmarks from existing file

2. **TestSaveBookmarks** (2 tests)
   - File creation
   - Valid JSON output

3. **TestAddBookmark** (3 tests)
   - Returns created bookmark
   - Persists to disk
   - Generates unique IDs

4. **TestDeleteBookmark** (2 tests)
   - Deletes existing bookmark
   - Returns False for missing ID

5. **TestGetBookmark** (2 tests)
   - Retrieves bookmark by ID
   - Returns None for missing ID

Uses pytest fixtures:
- `db_path`: temp file path for isolated testing
- `sample_bookmarks`: Two sample bookmarks with IDs "aaa-111" and "bbb-222"

---

**Test File 2: tests/test_search.py**

**CRITICAL:** These tests define expected behavior for a **NOT YET IMPLEMENTED** search feature. Tests import from `src.search` (which doesn't exist):

```python
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title
```

The tests expect 4 search functions:

1. **`search_by_title(bookmarks, query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
   - Case-insensitive substring matching
   - Test cases: "python" (2 results), "argparse" (1 result), "nonexistent" (0 results)

2. **`search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
   - Exact tag matching (not substring)
   - Test cases: "flask" (1), "docs" (3), "rust" (0)

3. **`search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
   - Domain extraction from URL and matching
   - Supports subdomain matching: "docs.python.org" matches 2 bookmarks
   - Test cases: "github.com" (2), "docs.python.org" (2), "stackoverflow.com" (0)

4. **`search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`**
   - Combined search with AND logic (all criteria must match)
   - Returns all bookmarks if no criteria provided
   - Returns empty list if criteria conflict
   - Test cases: single criteria (title/tag/domain), multiple criteria combinations

**CLI integration tests:**
- Expects a `search` subcommand in the parser
- Should accept `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain` options
- Should support `--format` option (json/table)

**Sample test data:** 5 bookmarks with various combinations of tags and domains

---

### 7. PROJECT CONFIGURATION (pyproject.toml)

```toml
Project name: bookmark-cli
Version: 1.0.0
Description: A CLI tool for managing a local bookmark collection
Python requirement: >=3.10
Entry point: bookmark = "src.cli:main"
Test dependencies: pytest>=7.4.0
Test paths: ["tests"]
```

---

## SUMMARY

**What exists:**
- Complete data model for bookmarks (6 fields)
- Functional storage layer with CRUD operations (JSON file based)
- CLI structure with 4 working commands (add, list, delete, show)
- Two output formatters (table, JSON)
- 11 passing storage tests

**What needs to be implemented:**
- `src/search.py` module with 4 search functions
- CLI integration of the search command with proper argument parsing
- Tests expect AND logic for multi-criteria searches

This is a well-structured, test-driven project ready for the search feature implementation.

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

"""Tests for search functionality.

These tests define the expected behaviour of the search module that needs
to be implemented in src/search.py and wired into the CLI.
"""

import json
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title


@pytest.fixture
def bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html",
            title="Python Tutorial",
            tags=["python", "tutorial", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python tutorial",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python",
            title="RunPod Python SDK",
            tags=["python", "sdk", "runpod"],
            notes="RunPod serverless SDK",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ccc-333",
            url="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript",
            title="MDN JavaScript Guide",
            tags=["javascript", "docs", "mdn"],
            notes="Comprehensive JS reference",
            created_at="2026-01-03T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ddd-444",
            url="https://github.com/pallets/flask",
            title="Flask Web Framework",
            tags=["python", "flask", "web"],
            notes="Lightweight WSGI web framework",
            created_at="2026-01-04T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="eee-555",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html",
            title="argparse documentation",
            tags=["python", "cli", "docs"],
            notes="Argparse module reference",
            created_at="2026-01-05T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


# ---------- search_by_title ----------


class TestSearchByTitle:
    def test_case_insensitive_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")
        titles = [b.title for b in result]
        assert "Python Tutorial" in titles
        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "eee-555"

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_tag ----------


class TestSearchByTag:
    def test_exact_tag_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_shared_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")
        assert len(result) == 3

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "rust")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_domain ----------


class TestSearchByDomain:
    def test_matches_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_matches_subdomain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "stackoverflow.com")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search (combined) ----------


class TestSearchCombined:
    def test_single_criterion_title(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_single_criterion_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, tag="javascript")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ccc-333"

    def test_single_criterion_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, domain="developer.mozilla.org")
        assert len(result) == 1

    def test_and_logic_title_and_tag(self, bookmarks):
        # "python" in title AND tagged "sdk"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "bbb-222"

    def test_and_logic_all_three(self, bookmarks):
        # title contains "python", tag "docs", domain "docs.python.org"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="docs", domain="docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"

    def test_no_criteria_returns_all(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks)
        assert len(result) == len(bookmarks)

    def test_conflicting_criteria_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask", tag="javascript")
        assert result == []


# ---------- CLI integration ----------


class TestSearchCLI:
    def test_search_command_exists(self):
        """The CLI parser should accept the 'search' command without error."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.title == "python"

    def test_search_format_json_flag(self):
        """The CLI should accept --format json on the search command."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.tag == "docs"
        assert args.format == "json"

"""Search functions for filtering bookmarks by various criteria."""

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains *query* (case-insensitive)."""
    query_lower = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if query_lower in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact match for *tag*."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL hostname matches *domain*."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).hostname == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Filter bookmarks by one or more criteria combined with AND logic.

    If no criteria are provided, all bookmarks are returned.
    """
    results = bookmarks

    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)

    return results

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
